topics: Function Definition (Prerequisite)|Injective Function (One-to-One)|Surjective Function (Onto)|Bijective Function (One-to-One Correspondence)|Inverse Functions|Cardinality of Sets|Composition of Bijections
parody_lyrics:
[Verse 1: Liam - Function Definition]
You're a function, defined on a set
Each x maps to just one y, you can bet
Don't need special conditions for (Huh)
Just mapping an element, that's enough

[Pre-Chorus: Harry - Introducing Surjective/Injective concepts by contrast]
Some outputs might not get hit by an input
Not every element's true

[Chorus: All & Harry - Defining Surjective, Injective, Bijective]
Oh, this function from domain A to set B
Let's check properties, see if you're unique
If each value in the codomain gets hit
Then you're surjective, oh-oh, you're mapping right
If only one input can reach its output
Then you're injective, and there is no doubt
With both properties present, it's plain to see
You're a bijection, oh-oh, one-to-one and onto
That's what makes you bijective

[Verse 2: Zayn - Inverse Functions, Cardinality]
If a function is bijective and true
An inverse function will exist for you
From B back to A, it's clear and bright
A one-to-one correspondence, shining light

[Pre-Chorus: Harry - Cardinality]
If a bijection is seen, it's plain to see
Cardinalities agree

[Chorus: All & Harry - Repetition of Bijective definition]
Oh, this function from domain A to set B
Let's check properties, see if you're unique
If each value in the codomain gets hit
Then you're surjective, oh-oh, you're mapping right
If only one input can reach its output
Then you're injective, and there is no doubt
With both properties present, it's plain to see
You're a bijection, oh-oh, one-to-one and onto
That's what makes you bijective

[Bridge: All - Composition of Bijections]
If F and G are both bijective, see
Their composition's neat
It will also be bijective, you'll agree
The proof is complete

[Breakdown: Harry - Reinforcing Bijective Properties]
Oh, this function from domain A to set B
An injective mapping, no overlap found
With surjectivity, all outputs are bound
(You don't know, oh-oh) You're a bijection, profound

[Chorus: All, Harry & Zayn - Final Repetition and Summary]
Oh, this function from domain A to set B
An injective mapping (c'mon), no overlap found
With surjectivity (every output hit), all are bound
You're a bijection, oh-oh, truly profound (Oh, woah)
If only you saw what a bijection means
It defines equivalence, between two domains
How direct your mapping, oh-oh, makes us believe
You're a bijection (bi-jection), oh-oh, one-to-one and onto
You're a bijection, oh-oh, it's so clear
That's what makes you bijective

topics: Cardinality of Infinite Sets|Power Set|Bijective Functions|Injective Functions|Cantor's Diagonal Argument|Schröder–Bernstein Theorem|Construction of Bijections
parody_lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The world of sets
We can't just count them one by one
Infinite size can be so grand
With one to one
Mappings come so very clear
For cardinality's true test

[Pre-Chorus]
Map
A function maps
Relate a set
An arrow from A to B

[Chorus]
Sweet Cardinality
New understanding felt so right
When Cantor showed
Infinite is not all the same
Oh, it's true

[Verse 2]
Take a power set
It's all subsets you could form from it
The set of N's power P of N
Is bigger than
The N's cardinality
Cantor diagonal showed us how

[Pre-Chorus]
Maps
One to one surjective
A bijection then
Shows sets are equivalent

[Chorus]
Sweet Cardinality
With P of N and then the Reals
A bijection built
To show they're both the same in size
We know it's true

[Instrumental Break / Mathematical Interlude]

[Chorus]
Sweet Cardinality
For injections one to one
Sweet Cardinality
A surjection means it's on to
Sweet Cardinality
Schroder Bernstein comes to help
